description: TODO Triage — small bug fixes and minor editor features triaged from references/TODO.md: suggestion-mode state, editor save round-trip, margin alignment, aspect picker, auto-typography, vim clipboard toggle.

userStories.title: Move suggestion currentFragmentUUID from manifest to DB
userStories.description: As a developer, I need the suggestion-mode current-fragment pointer stored as DB state, not in the project manifest, so it stops churning project.json and stops causing stale reads (root cause of the back-nav bug).
acceptanceCriteria: Add a per-project state table (or a column on an existing per-project state table) in the vault DB to hold currentFragmentUUID; generate and run the migration successfully
The suggestion read/write path in packages/storage/src/service/storage-service.ts reads and updates currentFragmentUUID from the DB, not the manifest
Remove currentFragmentUUID from the project manifest schema (packages/shared/src/schemas/domain/project.ts); project.json no longer carries or rewrites it on every suggestion advance
Run bun run codegen so the regenerated OpenAPI/frontend types drop the manifest field
Document the pointer as DB-only state (not re-derivable from the vault) in specifications/storage-sync.md and remove it from specifications/project-config.md; append a Shipped entry to both
Tests pass (pointer persists across requests via the DB)
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Fix suggestion back-navigation landing on the wrong fragment
userStories.description: As a writer in suggestion mode, when I navigate away from a suggested fragment and back, I want to return to the exact fragment I left, never its predecessor.
acceptanceCriteria: Navigating away from a suggestion and back returns to the exact fragment last shown, never its predecessor
The current-suggestion pointer (now DB-backed per US-001) is updated synchronously when the displayed fragment changes — no off-by-one between display and persisted pointer
Add a test covering away->back returns the same fragment
Update specifications/prompting.md (and navigation.md if back-nav is specced there) if a behavior clarification is needed; append a Shipped entry
Tests pass
Typecheck passes
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill
userStories.notes: Depends on US-001 — the manifest pointer is the suspected cause.

userStories.title: Edit counter should count changed sessions, not every save
userStories.description: As a writer, I want the suggestion-mode edit counter (fragment editCount) to increment only for editing sessions that actually changed the fragment, not on every save.
acceptanceCriteria: A save with no content change does not increment fragment editCount
editCount increments at most once per suggestion-mode visit (fragment open -> navigate away) in which at least one save changed content
The increment is tied to the existing fragment-visit boundary, not the save handler
Document the counting rule in specifications/prompting.md (and project-statistics.md / storage-sync.md where editCount semantics live); append a Shipped entry
Add tests: no-op save -> no increment; changed save -> +1; repeated changed saves within one session -> +1
Tests pass
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Save must not mutate whitespace or jump the cursor
userStories.description: As a writer, I want saving after an edit to be deterministic — it must not strip whitespace or move my cursor — so saving never feels like data loss.
acceptanceCriteria: Saving never moves the cursor; the caret stays at its pre-save position
When the save response is byte-equal to the editor content, the editor content state is NOT re-set from the response (no remount, no caret reset)
Whitespace handling on save is deterministic with no run-to-run variance; preserve trailing whitespace unless an existing intended normalization is found (record the decision in the spec)
Behavior is identical across rich, raw, and vim modes
Update specifications/fragment-editor.md with the save round-trip contract (no content/caret mutation on an unchanged save); append a Shipped entry
Add a test: save with unchanged content does not alter the buffer or caret
Tests pass
Typecheck passes
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill

userStories.title: Leading blank lines must not desync Margin alignment
userStories.description: As a writer, when a fragment starts with one or more blank lines, I want the Margin column to stay aligned with the blocks it annotates instead of being vertically offset.
acceptanceCriteria: Leading blank lines at the top of a fragment do not desync the Margin column; row N sits beside block N
The editor-driven block geometry / origin-alignment derivation accounts for leading empty blocks
Adding/removing leading blank lines live-reconciles the alignment
Works in both rich (Tiptap) and raw/vim (CM6) modes
Update specifications/margins.md if the alignment contract needs to state leading-whitespace handling; append a Shipped entry
Add or extend a unit test for block-index alignment with leading blank blocks
Tests pass
Typecheck passes
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill

userStories.title: Enter in the aspect picker selects the highlighted option
userStories.description: As a writer adding an aspect, I want Enter to select the highlighted existing option instead of creating a new aspect, so I stop creating accidental duplicate aspects.
acceptanceCriteria: With a highlighted option in the aspect picker, Enter selects the highlighted option and does not create a new aspect
Create-new-aspect fires only when no option is highlighted, or via an explicit 'Create <query>' affordance
Behavior is consistent across every aspect-add surface (fragment metadata editor TagCombobox and any other entry point)
Update specifications/aspect-arc-model.md (or fragment-editor.md if the picker behavior is documented there) if a clarification is needed; append a Shipped entry
Add a test: Enter selects highlighted vs creates when none highlighted
Tests pass
Typecheck passes
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill

userStories.title: Automatic typographic substitution in the rich editor
userStories.description: As a writer, I want common typographic substitutions (-- to em dash, ... to ellipsis, straight to curly quotes) applied as I type in the rich editor so my prose reads like a finished document.
acceptanceCriteria: Enable Tiptap's Typography extension in rich (Tiptap) mode: -- -> em dash, ... -> ellipsis, straight -> curly quotes (the extension's standard input-rule set)
Substitution is always on in rich mode (no setting); raw markdown and vim modes are untouched
Substituted characters are stored as the actual Unicode glyph in the saved markdown and round-trip byte-stable through save/load
Code spans / code blocks are excluded from substitution
Update specifications/fragment-editor.md to document rich-mode typography substitution; append a Shipped entry
Typecheck passes
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill

userStories.title: Per-project toggle for vim yank/delete to system clipboard
userStories.description: As a vim user, I want a per-project setting controlling whether vim y/d also write to the system clipboard, so Maskor does not clobber my OS clipboard when I prefer vim registers only.
acceptanceCriteria: Add a per-project boolean in project.json editor config gating vim yank/delete -> system-clipboard sync; default on
When off, vim yank/delete affect only vim registers and leave the system clipboard untouched
When on, y/yy/Y and d/dd/D/x mirror affected text to the system clipboard; vim register behavior and p/P paste semantics are unchanged
Expose the toggle in the editor 'Aa' display popover / project-config editor section
Run bun run codegen if the project-config schema is part of the API surface
Update specifications/fragment-editor.md and specifications/project-config.md to document the setting; append a Shipped entry
Typecheck passes
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill
userStories.notes: Coordinate with prd-small-improvements.md US-002 (vim clipboard sync) — fold together if that story is still unshipped.
